records.ety: [L. civilitas: cf. F. civilit. See Civil.]

defs: The state of society in which the relations and duties of a citizen are recognized and obeyed; a state of civilization.
quotes: Monarchies have risen from barbarrism to civility, and fallen again to ruin.
The gradual depature of all deeper signification from the word civility has obliged the creation of another word -- civilization.

defs: A civil office, or a civil process
Courtesy; politeness; kind attention; good breeding; a polite act or expression.
quotes: The insolent civility of a proud man is, if possible, more shocking than his rudeness could be.
